svg.title: Hi-C as covariance — full algebraic pipeline from balanced contacts to compartment labels
svg.desc: A four-stage horizontal pipeline. Stage 1: balanced contact matrix C with a TAD-and-checkerboard pattern. Arrow labelled "divide by expected by distance" leads to Stage 2: the observed/expected matrix with the diagonal flat and the compartment checkerboard sharper. Arrow labelled "row-wise Pearson correlation" leads to Stage 3: the correlation matrix R with the bipartite block structure unambiguously visible. Arrow labelled "first eigenvector" leads to Stage 4: a 1D track of eigenvector signs along the chromosome with alternating positive (cobalt, A) and negative (amber, B) regions. Below each stage a one-line equation: C, OE = C / E(|i-j|), R = corr(OE), v_1 such that R v_1 = lambda_1 v_1.

g.text: HI-C AS COVARIANCE — FROM BALANCED CONTACTS TO COMPARTMENT LABELS
Four linear-algebra moves, none specific to genomics.
Each stage is one operator from any linear-algebra course: a matrix divide, a Pearson correlation, an eigendecomposition. None has biology-specific subtlety.
The genomics-specific choices are upstream (which bins go in, how E(|i−j|) is estimated) and downstream (which sign convention assigns A vs B).
Same math as eigenfaces, sensor-array beamforming, and inertia-tensor principal axes.
